description: Complete ADR-2603282000. P1 (MCP server), P2 (boundary enforcement), P3 (agentic loop), P5 (spawn endpoint), and P8-partial (coordination tests) are already implemented. This workplan covers the remaining phases: P4 (Dockerfile + sandbox.yml), P6 (hook worktree gate), P7 (hex swarm microVM-first), and P8-completion (MCP server tests).

tasks.name: hex-agent/Dockerfile — multi-stage build
tasks.description: Create hex-agent/Dockerfile with two stages. Stage 1 (builder): FROM rust:1-slim, install build deps (pkg-config, libssl-dev), COPY workspace Cargo files + hex-agent + hex-core source, RUN cargo build --release -p hex-agent. Stage 2 (runtime): FROM debian:bookworm-slim, install ca-certificates + git, COPY --from=builder the hex-agent binary to /usr/local/bin/hex-agent, set ENV WORKSPACE=/workspace, WORKDIR /workspace, ENTRYPOINT ["hex-agent", "daemon"]. The 'daemon' subcommand should be the existing daemon mode (run_loop). Done when: docker build -t hex-agent:latest hex-agent/ succeeds (or cargo build -p hex-agent passes as build gate).

tasks.name: hex-agent/sandbox.yml — Docker AI Sandbox network policy
tasks.description: Create hex-agent/sandbox.yml defining the Docker AI Sandbox microVM config. Fields: name: hex-agent-sandbox, image: hex-agent:latest, network.allow list: host.docker.internal:3033 (SpacetimeDB), host.docker.internal:5555 (hex-nexus REST), host.docker.internal:11434 (Ollama), openrouter.ai:443, crates.io:443, static.crates.io:443, npmjs.com:443. Add a comment explaining the Linux fallback (SPACETIMEDB_HOST injected by nexus for non-macOS). Done when: file is valid YAML and documents all required allow-list entries from the ADR.

tasks.name: hex hook subagent-start — worktree enforcement gate
tasks.description: In hex-cli/src/commands/hook.rs (or wherever 'subagent-start' is handled), add the worktree gate: (1) Read HEXFLO_TASK env var — if not set, exit 0 immediately (interactive session, no gate). (2) If HEXFLO_TASK is set, detect if cwd is the project root by checking if cwd/Cargo.toml contains '[workspace]'. (3) Also check if cwd path contains 'hex-worktrees' or '/feat/' — if so, it's a worktree, exit 0. (4) If cwd is project root and HEXFLO_TASK is set: print to stderr 'worktree_required: HEXFLO_TASK is set but cwd is project root — swarm agents must run in a git worktree (ADR-004)' and exit with code 1. Unit tests: HEXFLO_TASK unset → exit 0; HEXFLO_TASK set + worktree cwd → exit 0; HEXFLO_TASK set + project root → exit 1 with worktree_required message. Done when: cargo test -p hex-cli passes for the new hook tests.

tasks.name: Docker availability check + docker run spawn in agent_manager
tasks.description: In hex-nexus/src/orchestration/agent_manager.rs spawn_agent(), add Docker-first logic: (1) Check if docker is available: run 'docker info --format json' with timeout 2s. (2) If available AND worktree_branch is set AND hex-agent:latest image exists (docker image inspect hex-agent:latest): spawn via docker run --rm -d -e WORKSPACE=/workspace -e HEX_NEXUS_URL -e HEX_AGENT_ID -e HEXFLO_TASK -e SPACETIMEDB_TOKEN --mount type=bind,src=<worktree_path>,dst=/workspace hex-agent:latest. Return container_id as the agent handle. (3) If docker unavailable or image missing: log 'docker_unavailable: falling back to process spawn' and use the existing process spawn path unchanged. No new dependencies needed (std::process::Command for docker). Done when: cargo check -p hex-nexus passes; unit test verifies docker-unavailable path falls through to process spawn.

tasks.name: Unit tests for mcp_server.rs — path safety and boundary enforcement
tasks.description: In hex-agent/src/adapters/primary/mcp_server.rs, add #[cfg(test)] block with: (1) safe_path_blocks_traversal: call safe_path('../../etc/passwd', '/workspace') in a temp dir, assert Err containing 'path_traversal_rejected'. (2) safe_path_allows_valid: safe_path('src/lib.rs', workspace_root) returns Ok. (3) check_boundary_primary_importing_secondary: call check_boundary('src/adapters/primary/foo.rs', 'use crate::adapters::secondary::Bar;'), assert Some(msg) containing 'boundary_violation'. (4) check_boundary_clean_file: check_boundary on a domain file with no adapter imports returns None. (5) handle_request_initialize: call handle_request with initialize JSON, assert response contains 'serverInfo'. (6) handle_request_tools_list: call handle_request with tools/list, assert response contains 'hex_write_file'. Done when: cargo test -p hex-agent passes for these 6 tests.
